content.text: Now let me process all 320 records and compute aging from `time_received` as of 2026-06-12.

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,glob,datetime
from datetime import timezone

files=glob.glob("/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/1e45db10-860e-4bcd-818b-827d33e308b5/tool-results/mcp-mcp_d1c47102-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-*.txt")
recs={}
for f in files:
    d=json.load(open(f))
    inner=json.loads(d["result"])
    for r in inner["ap_invoices"]:
        recs[r["id"]]=r
# add offset300
d=json.load(open("/tmp/ap_offset300.json"))
for r in d["ap_invoices"]:
    recs.setdefault(r["id"],r)
print("unique pending:",len(recs))

TODAY=datetime.datetime(2026,6,12,tzinfo=timezone.utc)
def parse(s):
    if not s: return None
    try:
        return datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(s)
    except: return None

rows=[]
for r in recs.values():
    tr=parse(r.get("time_received"))
    inv=parse(r.get("invoice_date"))
    ref=tr or inv
    if ref is None: 
        age=None
    else:
        if ref.tzinfo is None: ref=ref.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        age=(TODAY-ref).days
    rows.append({"id":r["id"],"entity":r.get("entity_id"),"vendor":r.get("vendor_name"),
                 "vid":r.get("vendor_id"),"inv":r.get("invoice_number"),"amt":r.get("amount"),
                 "age":age,"approver":r.get("approver"),"received":r.get("time_received")})
rows.sort(key=lambda x:(x["age"] if x["age"] is not None else -1),reverse=True)

over60=[r for r in rows if r["age"] and r["age"]>=60]
over80=[r for r in rows if r["age"] and r["age"]>=80]
print("over60:",len(over60)," over80:",len(over80))
print("\n== AGE BUCKETS ==")
import collections
b=collections.Counter()
for r in rows:
    a=r["age"]
    if a is None: b["?"]+=1
    elif a>=80: b["80+"]+=1
    elif a>=60: b["60-79"]+=1
    elif a>=30: b["30-59"]+=1
    else: b["<30"]+=1
print(dict(b))
print("\n== ALL 80+ DAY (worst offenders) ==")
tot=0
for r in over80:
    tot+=r["amt"]
    print(f'{r["age"]:>4}d  {r["entity"]:<14} {r["vendor"]:<34} {r["inv"]:<16} ${r["amt"]:>11,.2f}  appr={r["approver"]}')
print(f"80+ total: ${tot:,.2f}  count={len(over80)}")

print("\n== 60-79 DAY ==")
tot2=0
for r in [x for x in rows if x['age'] and 60<=x['age']<80]:
    tot2+=r['amt']
    print(f'{r["age"]:>4}d  {r["entity"]:<14} {r["vendor"]:<34} {r["inv"]:<16} ${r["amt"]:>11,.2f}')
print(f"60-79 total: ${tot2:,.2f}")
EOF

content.text: This is a far larger backlog than a "few vendors" — **275 of 320 pending invoices are 80+ days old** (most ~320 days, dating to July 2025), and **every pending invoice has `approver=None`**. Total 80+ day exposure: **$6.13M**. Let me create tasks and investigate root causes for the worst offenders.

content.text:
Rich context. Key systemic finding from Slack: the backlog root cause is an **orphaned approval chain after a July 2025 staff departure** (invoices left with no assigned approver — matching `approver=None` across the whole queue). Mateo owns the routing fix + a brookfield-wide sweep. Daniel Jones = Accounts Manager (senior tier), Steven Perry = Managing Partner (partner sign-off).

Several specific items are already triaged. Let me now pull SOWs/engagement letters from Records Vault and check Linear + email for the big-dollar Acme/Northstar offenders.

\"Add the closeout summary for CrownPeak invoice VEN-030-736427 / apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 to the existing combined CrownPeak follow-up record so the three-of-three arc is documented in one place.\\n\\nContext:\\n- Manual review concluded this is not a duplicate or live billing dispute.\\n- The underlying Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer for northstar_legal was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when coverage moved in-house.\\n- Pre-bill was actually issued on 2026-03-11, about eleven days after the cancellation was logged, and is now 81 days old.\\n- Recommended disposition is void as stale on Brookfield's side.\\n- CrownPeak AR confirmed a manual credit memo will be backfilled within five business days.\\n- Daniel Jones is taking the partner follow-up to Matthew Li; Priya Khatri is drafting the AP-side policy framing on cancellation-triggered void scans.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n- Append the narrative disposition and source breadcrumbs from Slack/email to the existing CrownPeak follow-up record.\\n- Note that this closes the third of three CrownPeak items.\\n- Keep all three CrownPeak follow-ups consolidated on one record for future traceability.\", \"priority\": 3, \"estimate\": 2, \"due_date\": \"2025-10-03\", \"labels\": [\"northstar_legal\", \"ap-follow-up\", \"vendor-credit-memo\", \"documentation\"], \"links\": [], \"Finalize Pinecrest dispute notice conditions after partner approval\", \"description\": \"Partner approval received from Andrea Phil to proceed with a formal dispute and hold payment on Pinecrest Workflow Works invoice VEN-006-193120 / SAP invoice apinv_dff20c11abdc495c.\\n\\nConditions to carry into the notice:\\n- cite the provisioning-log dates reviewed\\n- cite telemetry seat counts showing 5 active users rather than 6 across the billed period\\n- give Pinecrest a 30-day response window ending 2026-06-21\\n- log the brookfield vendor-dispute tracker the same day outside this \"created_at\": \"2026-05-21T14:46:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-27T18:51:54Z\"}, {\"id\": \"issue_45d50dfc34c74996a477309304270f26\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"npc_029\", \"state_id\": \"todo\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Track Pinecrest VEN-006-193120 dispute for invoice apinv_dff20c11abdc495c\", \"description\": \"Daniel asked that this be opened for dispute tracking and broader Pinecrest license review.\\n\\nInvoice references\\n- AP invoice id: apinv_dff20c11abdc495c\\n- Vendor: Pinecrest Workflow Works\\n- Vendor invoice number: VEN-006-193120\\n- Client/entity context: brookfield\\n- Amount under review: $1,040.63\\n\\nContract decomposition supporting the billed amount\\n- Base contract: 5 seats at $208.12 per month\\n- Per-seat add: $208.13 per month\\n- Disputed billed amount of $1,040.63 matches 5 months of 1 additional seat billed for March through July 2025\\n\\nAudit findings\\n- IT provisioning review found no SSO grants for any additional Pinecrest seat during 2025\\n- Usage telemetry showed 5 active users across the full billed period, not 6\\n- Evidence supports that the billed incremental seat was not provisioned and not used\\n\\nRecommended disposition\\n- Escalate to vendor dispute\\n- Maintain payment hold on invoice apinv_dff20c11abdc495c pending vendor response and credit/rebill outcome\\n- Treat this as a service-delivery dispute rather than an internal approval-delay issue\\n\\nFollow-up required\\n- Complete a base-seat utilization audit for Pinecrest with due date 2026-06-12 to confirm whether the contracted 5-seat baseline remains appropriate going forward\\n\\nNote\\n- The Airtable vendor-dispute tracker will be appended outside this scenario.\", \"priority\": 2, \"estimate\": null, \"due_date\": \"2026-06-12\", \"labels\": [\"ap-escalation\", \"issue_05de9a8fc05c4effb88c2797c882ce41\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": null, \"state_id\": \"todo\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Review CrownPeak AP queue and tune duplicate detection for memo variance\", \"description\": \"Trigger invoice for northstar_legal: apinv_e0235997b4964a8f, CrownPeak Cyber Defense invoice VEN-030-353041 for $459.84, has been sitting pending approval since 2026-03-09 (83 days old as of the weekly exceptions scan) and appears to be a duplicate of already paid sibling invoice VEN-030-353009, paid on 2026-03-13 for the same March 2026 MSSP cycle. The sibling was paid four days after VEN-030-353041 was issued, indicating CrownPeak AR caught the duplicate but did not void the reissue cleanly on their side.\\n\\nVendor-side confirmation relayed internally indicates VEN-030-353041 was a billing-system reissue generated during CrownPeak's February 2026 platform migration (roughly three weeks before the duplicate was issued) and VEN-030-353009 was the valid invoice. No additional March billing is expected.\\n\\nControl gap observed: duplicate detection did not catch the sibling invoices because memo text differed materially enough to evade the current match logic. One memo reads \\\"Mar MSSP retainer\\\" and the other reads \\\"March MSSP monthly fee.\\\" This should be reviewed as a workflow/control issue, not only a one-off duplicate dismissal. Tariq's parallel-history pull on the CrownPeak vendor file backs up the duplicate finding.\\n\\nAlso review the two other long-pending CrownPeak items in the northstar_legal AP queue called out in the weekly exceptions review. Brenda's quick pass suggests they do not look like obvious duplicates, so they need separate aging and approval-path review.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n- Document the duplicate relationship between apinv_e0235997b4964a8f / VEN-030-353041 and paid sibling VEN-030-353009.\\n- Propose a duplicate-detection rule adjustment or fuzzy-match threshold change for memo-variance cases like \\\"Mar MSSP retainer\\\" vs \\\"March MSSP monthly fee\\\" for Priya to sign off on as rule owner.\\n- Review the two other long-pending CrownPeak invoices for northstar_legal and summarize whether they are approval bottlenecks, coding issues, or separate vendor follow-ups.\\n- Capture recommended AP queue cleanup steps for CrownPeak items so the same pattern is easier to detect going forward.\\n\\nRelated context from internal escalation is preserved in email thread email_scen_033_ap_escalation_0003 and vendor confirmation email_scen_033_ap_escalation_0004.\", \"priority\": 2, \"estimate\": 3, \"due_date\": \"2026-06-02\", \"labels\": [\"ap-controls\", \"duplicate-detection\", \"vendor-review\", \"northstar_legal\", \"crownpeak\"], \"links\": [], \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-05-20T13:23:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-27T18:44:07Z\"}, {\"id\": \"issue_08d1ddb3b3c9419faa29e94436004bd8\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": apinv_9aa666fc03424902 and complete expired W-9 sweep\", \"description\": \"Root cause: TimeLedger Nexus has an expired W-9 on file dated 2026-02-15, which is blocking payment release under firm policy.\\n\\nInvoice in scope: apinv_9aa666fc03424902\\nVendor invoice number: VEN-010-693199\\nVendor: TimeLedger Nexus\\nAmount: $21,777.01\\nEntity: acme_cloud\\nGL account: 210000\\nCurrent SAP status: pending_approval\\n\\nConditioned release path:\\n- Hold release unless updated W-9 is received by the hard date set through partner sign-off.\\n- Brenda Abbas reported a soft vendor commitment to provide the refreshed W-9 by 2026-05-29.\\n- If the updated W-9 is not received by the agreed deadline, keep the invoice blocked and escalate per AP management direction.\\n\\nControl follow-up:\\n- Mateo Kovac to run the broader vendor-master sweep for expired W-9s so this is not isolated to the TimeLedger Nexus record.\\n- Capture any additional vendors with stale tax documentation and route for remediation.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n1. Conditioned release decision for apinv_9aa666fc03424902 is documented with hard-date requirement.\\n2. Brenda's 2026-05-29 soft commitment is recorded in the ticket.\\n3. Contract decomposition supporting the billed amount
- Base contract: 5 seats at $208.12 per month
- Per-seat add: $208.13 per month
- Disputed billed amount of $1,040.63 matches 5 months of 1 additional seat billed for March through July 2025

Audit findings
- IT provisioning review found no SSO grants for any additional Pinecrest seat during 2025
- Usage telemetry showed 5 active users across the full billed period, not 6
- Evidence supports that the billed incremental seat was not provisioned and not used

- Treat this as a service-delivery dispute rather than an internal approval-delay issue

The sibling was paid four days after VEN-030-353041 was issued, indicating CrownPeak AR caught the duplicate but did not void the reissue cleanly on their side.

Vendor-side confirmation relayed internally indicates VEN-030-353041 was a billing-system reissue generated during CrownPeak's February 2026 platform migration (roughly three weeks before the duplicate was issued) and VEN-030-353009 was the valid invoice. No additional March billing is expected.

Control gap observed: duplicate detection did not catch the sibling invoices because memo text differed materially enough to evade the current match logic. One memo reads "Mar MSSP retainer" and the other reads "March MSSP monthly fee." This should be reviewed as a workflow/control issue, not only a one-off duplicate dismissal. Tariq's parallel-history pull on the CrownPeak vendor file backs up the duplicate finding.

Also review the two other long-pending CrownPeak items in the northstar_legal AP queue called out in the weekly exceptions review. Brenda's quick pass suggests they do not look like obvious duplicates, so they need separate aging and approval-path review.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document the duplicate relationship between apinv_e0235997b4964a8f / VEN-030-353041 and paid sibling VEN-030-353009.
- Propose a duplicate-detection rule adjustment or fuzzy-match threshold change for memo-variance cases like "Mar MSSP retainer" vs "March MSSP monthly fee" for Priya to sign off on as rule owner.
- Review the two other long-pending CrownPeak invoices for northstar_legal and summarize whether they are approval bottlenecks, coding issues, or separate vendor follow-ups.
- Capture recommended AP queue cleanup steps for CrownPeak items so the same pattern is easier to detect going forward.

content.text: Let me compute the tier breakdown (AP-manager ≤$10K / controller $10–50K / partner >$50K) for the aged buckets and list the partner-tier worst offenders.

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,glob,datetime
from datetime import timezone
recs={}
for f in glob.glob("/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/1e45db10-860e-4bcd-818b-827d33e308b5/tool-results/mcp-mcp_d1c47102-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-*.txt"):
    inner=json.loads(json.load(open(f))["result"])
    for r in inner["ap_invoices"]: recs[r["id"]]=r
for r in json.load(open("/tmp/ap_offset300.json"))["ap_invoices"]: recs.setdefault(r["id"],r)
TODAY=datetime.datetime(2026,6,12,tzinfo=timezone.utc)
def age(r):
    s=r.get("time_received") or r.get("invoice_date")
    dt=datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(s)
    if dt.tzinfo is None: dt=dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    return (TODAY-dt).days
rows=[{"e":r.get("entity_id"),"v":r.get("vendor_name"),"inv":r.get("invoice_number"),"amt":r["amount"],"a":age(r)} for r in recs.values()]
def tier(a):
    return "partner(>$50K)" if a>50000 else "controller($10-50K)" if a>=10000 else "apmgr(<$10K)"
import collections
for label,lo,hi in [("60+",60,10**9),("80+",80,10**9),("60-79",60,80)]:
    sub=[r for r in rows if lo<=r["a"]<hi]
    byt=collections.Counter(); amt=collections.Counter()
    for r in sub: byt[tier(r["amt"])]+=1; amt[tier(r["amt"])]+=r["amt"]
    print(f"\n=== {label} days: {len(sub)} invoices, ${sum(r['amt'] for r in sub):,.0f} ===")
    for t in ["partner(>$50K)","controller($10-50K)","apmgr(<$10K)"]:
        print(f"  {t:<22} {byt[t]:>3} inv  ${amt[t]:>14,.2f}")
print("\n=== TOP 20 partner-tier (>$50K) 80+ day offenders ===")
big=sorted([r for r in rows if r["a"]>=80 and r["amt"]>50000],key=lambda x:-x["amt"])
for r in big[:20]:
    print(f'  {r["a"]:>4}d  {r["e"]:<14} {r["v"]:<32} {r["inv"]:<16} ${r["amt"]:>13,.2f}')
print(f"\n  partner-tier(>$50K) 80+ count={len(big)} total=${sum(r['amt'] for r in big):,.2f}")
# entity split 80+
print("\n=== 80+ by entity ===")
ent=collections.Counter(); enta=collections.Counter()
for r in rows:
    if r["a"]>=80: ent[r["e"]]+=1; enta[r["e"]]+=r["amt"]
for e in ent: print(f"  {e:<16} {ent[e]:>3} inv  ${enta[e]:>14,.2f}")
EOF

input.payload:
**AP pending-approval backlog review — SAP queue aged vs 60-day SLA (as of 2026-06-12)**

Heads-up: the vendor calls aren't "two or three" — this is a systemic backlog. Pulled the full SAP `pending_approval` queue (320 invoices) and aged it by date received.

**Scale**
• **296 invoices past the 60-day SLA — $6.43M**
• **275 invoices in the 80+ day range — $6.13M** (most date to **July 2025**, ~320 days old)
• Every pending invoice has **approver = None**.

**Root cause (systemic):** orphaned approval chains. Approval routes pointed at staff who left (the **July 2025 departure**, and the **April 2026 senior-accountant resignation**); invoices were never reassigned, so they've sat unapproved. Already on record — Linear *"Fix AP routing rule for departed approvers and sweep orphaned pending approvals"* (LatticeHill VEN-033-86573 was the canary; Andrea approved release, Mateo owns the routing fix + sweep). This backlog is the un-swept tail of that issue, and it's **firm-wide, not just brookfield.**

**By approval tier (80+ day):**
• **Partner sign-off (>$50K): 34 invoices — $4.29M** ← the real risk
• Controller/senior ($10–50K): 60 invoices — $1.39M
• AP-manager (<$10K): 181 invoices — $0.45M

**By entity (80+ day):** acme_cloud **90 inv / $5.60M** · northstar_legal 75 inv / $0.27M · brookfield 110 inv / $0.25M. Dollar exposure is overwhelmingly **Acme Cloud**.

**Worst offenders (partner-tier, 80+ days — all Acme Cloud):**
1. VaultKey Access & Security — VEN-029-961721 — **$460,556** (136d)
2. AssurePath Audit Workflow — VEN-005-84026 — **$294,270** (135d)
3. CivicSquare Property Management — VEN-024-891664 — **$289,218** (298d)
4. Clearpoint Risk Brokers — VEN-018-693265 — **$224,864** (239d)
5. PensionBridge Benefits — VEN-034-341062 — **$185,873** (257d)
6. PensionBridge Benefits — VEN-034-175195 — **$183,069** (256d)
7. Northloop Fiber Networks — VEN-015-500420 — **$168,054** (126d)
8. Pine & Meridian Advisory — VEN-016-700307 — **$136,187** (195d)
9. Clearpoint Risk Brokers — VEN-018-966408 — **$136,919** (105d)
10. EntitySpring Practice Management — VEN-008-810974 — **$132,677** (301d)

**80+ day categorization (disputed / missing credit memo / orphaned):**
• **Orphaned approval chain** — the large majority, incl. *all* the partner-tier mega items above. No dispute or credit-memo correspondence exists in email/Slack for VaultKey, AssurePath, CivicSquare, Clearpoint, PensionBridge, Northloop, etc. → these are clean obligations stuck on routing. **Verify PO + release.**
• **Disputed deliverable** — Pinecrest VEN-006-193120 ($1,040.63, partner-approved dispute, hold to 2026-06-21); TimeLedger VEN-010-514242 ($24,475 — Phase-3 milestone dispute).
• **Missing credit memo** — TimeLedger VEN-010-514242 (release undisputed $17,825, **hold $6,650.25** pending memo); CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 ($422.32, retainer cancelled 2026-02-28, **stale-void**, vendor backfilling memo).
• **Duplicate / wrong-SOW (do not pay)** — CrownPeak VEN-030-353041 ($459.84, dup of paid VEN-030-353009); GraniteRack VEN-012-753165 ($39,091, billed off superseded SOW-2024-GR-rev3, **void-and-rebill**).
• **Vendor-master block** — TimeLedger VEN-010-693199 ($21,777, expired W-9, conditioned release).

**Records Vault scope check (Acme/Northstar):** the only contract artifact filed is the **Northstar Legal FY2026 Audit Engagement Letter** (doc_0036f5b991574808, restricted — that's *our* audit engagement with Northstar, not a vendor SOW). **No Acme Cloud engagement letter and no standalone vendor SOWs are in the Vault** — vendor SOWs (e.g. GraniteRack SOW-2025-GR-rev1) live in procurement/engagement files. So in-scope/amount validation on the big Acme bills can't be done from the Vault and needs the PO + procurement contract.

**Recommended routing (nothing routed yet — flagging first):**
• Partner-tier (>$50K, $4.29M, Acme-heavy) → **Steven Perry**, but only after PO/SOW scope confirmation.
• Controller-tier ($10–50K) → **Daniel Jones**.
• Hold all disputed / credit-memo / duplicate / W-9 items above — those should *not* go into a release batch.

**Where I need a PO-side confirm to unblock:** the partner-tier Acme items have no Vault contract to validate against. Can you confirm POs / contracted amounts for the top offenders — starting with **VaultKey VEN-029-961721 ($460K), AssurePath VEN-005-84026 ($294K), CivicSquare VEN-024-891664 ($289K), and the two PensionBridge bills ($369K combined)**? Once amounts are confirmed in scope I'll package them for Steven; anything that fails scope I'll route to dispute instead.
